# Benchmarking Harness

Benchmarks are the book's source of truth.

## Chapter Job

Define a harness that can compare runtimes, hardware, models, and settings
without changing the question each time.

## Harness Components

- Prompt dataset.
- Output length control.
- Concurrency driver.
- Streaming probe.
- Metrics collector.
- Result summarizer.
- Environment snapshot.
- Cost calculator.
- Plotting script.

## Benchmark Types

- Single request trace.
- Concurrency sweep.
- Prompt-length sweep.
- Output-length sweep.
- Quantization comparison.
- Prefix-cache experiment.
- Multi-tenant load test.
- Failure and retry drill.

## Source Link

This chapter should promote material from
`source-material/prior-inference-journal/days/day-003-vLLM-capacity-and-OOM/`,
`source-material/prior-inference-journal/days/day-007-vllm-runtime-probes/`,
and
`source-material/prior-inference-journal/days/day-009-latency-metrics-and-slo-lab/`.
